titleStmt.title: SII 9.703: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 703. (A.R. No. 181 of 1910.) ON A STONE SET UP UNDER A MARGOSA TREE ON THE WAY TO THE RIVER BHAVANI AT SIVAYAPALAIYAM, SATYAMANGALAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 1591, Saumya, Pushya, ba. 7 corresponding to A.D. 1670 January 3 monday (not verifiable), in the reign of the Mysore king Dēvarāja-Voḍeya, son of Dēvarāja-Voḍeya. The king is stated to have made a gift of the village Bestarapāḷya, surnamed Komārapura, for the service of the god Dhavaḷagiri-Komārasvāmi in Dūrvāsa-kshētra at the confluence of the rivers, Chintāmaṇī and Bhavānī. The village granted was situated in Karavaḷi-hōbaḷi in Satyamaṅgala-sthala, which was a sub-division of Uḍuvaṅka-nāḍu below the Yēṇūr pass.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīvijayādbhudayaśālivāha
naśakavaruṣa 1591 saṁda varttamāna
saumyasaṁvatsarada puṣya ba 7 lu Ātrēyagōtra
da Āpastaṁbhasūtrada śrīmadrājādhirāja rāja
paramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa rāja śrī dēvarājavoḍe
yara Ayyanavara komārarāda dēvarājavaḍeyaraiyya
navaru maisūra siṁhāsanārūḍharāgi pruthvīrājyaṁgai
Uttiralu yēṇūra kaṇive keḷagaṇa Uḍuvaṁkanāḍa
sattyamaṁgalada staḷake saluva karavaḷi hōbaḷiyalli besta
rapāḷyake pratināmavāda komārapurada grāmavaṁnu
ciṁtāmaṇi bhāvani saṁgamadalli dūrvāsakṣētrada bī
ḍinalli dhavaḷagiri komārasvāmiyavara sēvege salila
navāgi koṭṭa bhūdāna grāmaśilāśāsana .
dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dānāchrēyōnupālanaṁ
dānātsvargamavāpnōti pālanādaccutaṁ padaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 9.703 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
